steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the best description of Sir Malcolm Vale based on the text. The passage notes his impressive, bust-worthy features and bulging forehead, but then sarcastically questions his "actual achievements" after fifty years of service — signaling that his appearance of distinction is not backed up by clear accomplishment.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — He looks like a person worthy of respect, but his memoirs reveal that some of his actions were dishonorable.: The passage mentions Vale's memoirs, but it does not say they reveal dishonorable actions. ✗
  • Choice B — He has maintained a modest profile even though he has served the public capably for many years.: The passage describes an imposing, not modest, profile, and his capability is precisely what the sarcasm calls into question. ✗
  • Choice C — He has the appearance of a distinguished figure, but it is uncertain whether he has accomplished anything to earn distinction.: This matches both the description of his impressive features and the sarcastic doubt about his actual achievements. ✓
  • Choice D — He would be a celebrated public figure if his achievements did not have to be kept secret.: Secrecy is never implied; the sarcasm suggests the achievements may simply not exist, not that they are hidden. ✗
That is the correct answer.
